reason: A crushing, methodical descent into primal dread that makes every shadow feel threatening.
Unflinching, savage satire that plunges you into a violent spiral you can’t shake off.
Psychological horror that burrows into your mind, leaving an unnerving sense of emptiness.
Claustrophobic sci-fi terror that taps into a deep, inescapable existential fear of the unknown.
Space horror so disturbing it lingers like a nightmare you can’t wake from.
Black-and-white zombie terror that still evokes a raw, intangible dread decades later.
A simple beach day twisted into primal fear of the unseen stalking the deep.
An urban legend come to life that haunts you long after the credits roll.
Meta-slasher that sinks its claws into a lingering fear of being watched and hunted.
Chilling cat-and-mouse with Hannibal that leaves a cavernous dread in the wake of its quiet menace.
